_comment: Curated ag and economic quotes for AGSIST Daily briefing. Generator picks from this pool — never synthesizes. Fail-loud on missing attribution. Remove any quote that becomes overused; add new ones freely. Structure: {text, attribution, tags}. Tags drive mood-aware selection in v4.2.

quotes.text: Farming looks mighty easy when your plow is a pencil and you're a thousand miles from the corn field.
The farmer is the only man in our economy who buys everything at retail, sells everything at wholesale, and pays the freight both ways.
Agriculture is our wisest pursuit, because it will in the end contribute most to real wealth, good morals, and happiness.
The discovery of agriculture was the first big step toward a civilized life.
If agriculture goes wrong, nothing else will have a chance to go right.
I believe in the future of agriculture, with a faith born not of words but of deeds.
You have to know the past to understand the present.
Without agriculture it is not possible to have a city, stock market, banks, university, church or army.
The ultimate goal of farming is not the growing of crops, but the cultivation and perfection of human beings.
To forget how to dig the earth and to tend the soil is to forget ourselves.
We abuse land because we regard it as a commodity belonging to us. When we see land as a community to which we belong, we may begin to use it with love and respect.
The soil is the great connector of our lives, the source and destination of all.
Eating is an agricultural act.
A good farmer is nothing more nor less than a handy man with a sense of humus.
Food is the moral right of all who are born into this world.
You can't eat potential.
Take good care of the earth. It was not given to you by your parents. It was loaned to you by your children.
Learn from yesterday, live for today, hope for tomorrow.
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
In seed time learn, in harvest teach, in winter enjoy.
Agriculture is the most healthful, most useful and most noble employment of man.
I'd rather be on my farm than be emperor of the world.
Never invest in a business you cannot understand.
The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
Price is what you pay. Value is what you get.
Risk comes from not knowing what you're doing.
In the business world, the rearview mirror is always clearer than the windshield.
The markets can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
When the facts change, I change my mind. What do you do, sir?
The four most dangerous words in investing are: this time it's different.
How did you go bankrupt? Two ways. Gradually, then suddenly.
quotes.attribution: Ernest Hemingway, The Sun Also Rises

quotes.text: If a problem cannot be solved, enlarge it.
Plans are nothing; planning is everything.
The best fertilizer on any farm is the footsteps of the farmer.
Good weather is the greatest gift in farming, and it cannot be purchased.
Don't judge each day by the harvest you reap but by the seeds that you plant.
Hard work beats talent when talent doesn't work hard.
The frost is on the pumpkin and the fodder's in the shock.
It is only the farmer who faithfully plants seeds in the spring, who reaps a harvest in the autumn.
The glory of gardening: hands in the dirt, head in the sun, heart with nature.
The farmer has to be an optimist or he wouldn't still be a farmer.
Buy land, they ain't making any more of it.
The worst thing about being wrong is the lesson you learn.
Good judgment comes from experience, and experience comes from bad judgment.
The more I want to get something done, the less I call it work.
It is better to hear the rebuke of the wise than for a man to hear the song of fools.
To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven: a time to plant, and a time to pluck up that which is planted.
He that observeth the wind shall not sow; and he that regardeth the clouds shall not reap.
The land is the only thing in the world worth working for, worth fighting for, worth dying for, because it's the only thing that lasts.
quotes.attribution: Gerald O'Hara, Gone With The Wind

quotes.text: The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.
He who plants a tree plants a hope.
Successful farming requires not the abandonment but the creative development of tradition.
A farmer is a magician who produces money from mud.
We are stuck with technology when what we really want is just stuff that works.
Never memorize something that you can look up.
The most dangerous phrase in the language is: we've always done it this way.
Cash is king, but cash flow is the kingdom.
Don't put all your eggs in one basket.
Many a mickle makes a muckle.
Penny wise and pound foolish.
A stitch in time saves nine.
Make hay while the sun shines.
Red sky at night, shepherd's delight; red sky in morning, shepherd's warning.
When March blows its horn, your barn will be filled with hay and corn.
A good farmer never dies; he just goes back to the soil.
The discovery of a new dish confers more happiness on humanity than the discovery of a new star.
Beware of little expenses. A small leak will sink a great ship.
An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
Well done is better than well said.
By failing to prepare, you are preparing to fail.
Diligence is the mother of good luck.
Lost time is never found again.
It is a mistake to try to look too far ahead. The chain of destiny can only be grasped one link at a time.
However beautiful the strategy, you should occasionally look at the results.
The pessimist sees difficulty in every opportunity. The optimist sees opportunity in every difficulty.
Success is stumbling from failure to failure with no loss of enthusiasm.
You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
A rising tide lifts all boats.
The ability to simplify means to eliminate the unnecessary so that the necessary may speak.
What gets measured gets managed.
The best way to predict the future is to create it.
Culture eats strategy for breakfast.
In God we trust. All others must bring data.
The care of the Earth is our most ancient and most worthy and, after all, our most pleasing responsibility.
A good farmer who is dedicated to that work makes a kind of music in the daily round.
We learn from our gardens to deal with the most urgent question of the time: How much is enough?
Land is a community is the basic concept of ecology, but that land is to be loved and respected is an extension of ethics.
We can be ethical only in relation to something we can see, feel, understand, love, or otherwise have faith in.
There are two spiritual dangers in not owning a farm. One is the danger of supposing that breakfast comes from the grocery, and the other that heat comes from the furnace.
The nation behaves well if it treats the natural resources as assets which it must turn over to the next generation increased, and not impaired, in value.
Far and away the best prize that life has to offer is the chance to work hard at work worth doing.
Education is the key to unlock the golden door of freedom.
Where there is no vision, there is no hope.
Success is to be measured not so much by the position that one has reached in life as by the obstacles which he has overcome.
What is the use of a house if you haven't got a tolerable planet to put it on?
We can never have enough of nature.
The first farmer was the first man, and all historic nobility rests on possession and use of land.
Don't be too timid and squeamish about your actions. All life is an experiment.
It ain't what you don't know that gets you into trouble. It's what you know for sure that just ain't so.
Buy land — they're not making it anymore.
The big money is not in the buying and selling, but in the waiting.
Knowing what you don't know is more useful than being brilliant.
It's remarkable how much long-term advantage people like us have gotten by trying to be consistently not stupid, instead of trying to be very intelligent.
The real key to making money in stocks is not to get scared out of them.
Know what you own, and know why you own it.
It was never my thinking that made the big money for me. It always was my sitting.
Markets are never wrong; opinions often are.
An economist's guess is liable to be just as good as anybody else's.
Even if you're on the right track, you'll get run over if you just sit there.
Nothing in this world can take the place of persistence.
The wise man puts all his eggs in one basket and watches the basket.
If you desire peace, cultivate justice, but at the same time cultivate the fields to produce more bread.
If we throw nature out the window, she comes back in the door with a pitchfork.
A garden requires patient labor and attention. Plants do not grow merely to satisfy ambitions or to fulfill good intentions.
In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
He who knows when he can fight and when he cannot, will be victorious.
Bottoms in the investment world don't end with four-year lows; they end with 10- or 15-year lows.
It's tough to make predictions, especially about the future.
When you come to a fork in the road, take it.
Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement. Nothing can be done without hope and confidence.
When one tugs at a single thing in nature, he finds it attached to the rest of the world.
You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
Don't count your chickens before they hatch.
The early bird catches the worm.
A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
Without a struggle, there can be no progress.
It is thus with farming: if you do one thing late, you will be late in all your work.
The forefathers praised a farm when it had a good neighbor.
Praise large farms, but cultivate a small one.
The farmer's year is a circle, and his work is never done.
Observe due measure, for right timing is in all things the most important factor.
Do not put all your goods in one ship.
Agriculture is the mother and nurse of the other arts.
Of all the occupations by which gain is secured, none is better than agriculture, none more profitable, none more delightful, none more becoming to a free man.
While the farmer holds the title to the land, actually it belongs to all the people because civilization itself rests upon the soil.
Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
It is not because things are difficult that we do not dare; it is because we do not dare that things are difficult.
No man was ever wise by chance.
It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.
The best revenge is not to be like your enemy.
Confine yourself to the present.
Those who labor in the earth are the chosen people of God, if ever He had a chosen people.
The greatest service which can be rendered any country is to add a useful plant to its culture.
I am entirely a farmer, soul and body, never scarcely admitting a sentiment on any other subject.
I know of no pursuit in which more real and important services can be rendered to any country than by improving its agriculture.
I shall prepare and perhaps my time will come.
Give me six hours to chop down a tree and I will spend the first four sharpening the axe.
Things may come to those who wait, but only the things left by those who hustle.
Population must increase rapidly, more rapidly than in former times, and ere long the most valuable of all arts will be the art of deriving a comfortable subsistence from the smallest area of soil.
No other human occupation opens so wide a field for the profitable and agreeable combination of labor with cultivated thought as agriculture.
An ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure.
Plough deep while sluggards sleep, and you shall have corn to sell and to keep.
He that can have patience can have what he will.
Energy and persistence conquer all things.
Don't gamble; take all your savings and buy some good stock and hold it till it goes up, then sell it. If it don't go up, don't buy it.
Good judgment comes from experience, and a lot of that comes from bad judgment.
Buy land. They ain't making any more of the stuff.
The time to repair the roof is when the sun is shining.
Farmers are the only indispensable people on the face of the earth.
Plans are worthless, but planning is everything.
The nation that destroys its soil destroys itself.
It is common sense to take a method and try it. If it fails, admit it frankly and try another. But above all, try something.
Far better is it to dare mighty things, to win glorious triumphs, even though checkered by failure, than to rank with those poor spirits who neither enjoy much nor suffer much.
Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.
Comparison is the thief of joy.
The only thing new in the world is the history you do not know.
If you can't stand the heat, get out of the kitchen.
No person was ever honored for what he received. Honor has been the reward for what he gave.
Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn.
Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
Heaven is under our feet as well as over our heads.
Success usually comes to those who are too busy to be looking for it.
What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving your goals.
Convictions are more dangerous foes of truth than lies.
October: this is one of the peculiarly dangerous months to speculate in stocks. The others are July, January, September, April, November, May, March, June, December, August, and February.
The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
Climate is what we expect, weather is what we get.
Everybody talks about the weather, but nobody does anything about it.
A man of words and not of deeds is like a garden full of weeds.
If a man loses pace with his companions, perhaps it is because he hears a different drummer.
To plant a garden is to believe in tomorrow.
When tillage begins, other arts follow. The farmers, therefore, are the founders of human civilization.
Let us never forget that the cultivation of the earth is the most important labor of man.
There seem to be but three ways for a nation to acquire wealth: war, commerce, and agriculture. The first is robbery, the second cheating, and the third the only honest way.
No occupation is so delightful to me as the culture of the earth, and no culture comparable to that of the garden.
If we do not permit the earth to produce beauty and joy, it will in the end not produce food, either.
The soil is the great connector of lives, the source and destination of all.
I would feel more optimistic about a bright future for man if he spent less time proving that he can outwit Nature and more time tasting her sweetness and respecting her seniority.
In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.
When we try to pick out anything by itself, we find it hitched to everything else in the universe.
A society grows great when old men plant trees whose shade they know they shall never sit in.
Cast your bread upon the waters, for you will find it after many days.
Whoever watches the wind will not plant; whoever looks at the clouds will not reap.
As you sow, so shall you reap.
The farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient about it, until it receives the early and the late rains.
He who works his land will have abundant food, but he who chases fantasies lacks judgment.
Education is the ability to listen to almost anything without losing your temper or your self-confidence.
The best way out is always through.
In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on.
Farming is a profession of hope.
The master's eye is the best fertilizer.
Fortune favors the bold.
They can because they think they can.
However difficult life may seem, there is always something you can do and succeed at.
Out of clutter, find simplicity. From discord, find harmony. In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
Look deep into nature, and then you will understand everything better.
Life is like riding a bicycle. To keep your balance you must keep moving.
The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
A journey of a thousand miles must begin with a single step.
He who knows that enough is enough will always have enough.
When the root is deep, there is no reason to fear the wind.
Fall seven times, stand up eight.
Vision without action is a daydream. Action without vision is a nightmare.
No man ever stepped in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man.
The only constant in life is change.
Character is destiny.
Quality is not an act, it is a habit.
Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
What does not kill me makes me stronger.
Knowing trees, I understand the meaning of patience. Knowing grass, I can appreciate persistence.
No winter lasts forever; no spring skips its turn.
Anyone who thinks gardening begins in the spring and ends in the fall is missing the best part of the whole year.
The love of dirt is among the earliest of passions, as it is the latest.
What is a weed? A plant whose virtues have not yet been discovered.
Why try to explain miracles to your kids when you can just have them plant a garden.
Despite all our accomplishments we owe our existence to a six-inch layer of topsoil and the fact that it rains.
quotes.attribution: Farm Equipment Association of Minnesota and South Dakota

quotes.text: When the well is dry, we know the worth of water.
Land, then, is not merely soil; it is a fountain of energy flowing through a circuit of soils, plants, and animals.
There is nothing new in Wall Street. There can't be because speculation is as old as the hills. Whatever happens in the stock market today has happened before and will happen again.
The big money is not in the buying and the selling, but in the waiting.
It is remarkable how much long-term advantage people like us have gotten by trying to be consistently not stupid, instead of trying to be very intelligent.
The main thing about money is to keep it. The second thing is to make it.
Don't try to buy at the bottom and sell at the top. It can't be done except by liars.
The main purpose of the stock market is to make fools of as many men as possible.
In the short run, the market is a voting machine but in the long run, it is a weighing machine.
The investor's chief problem — and even his worst enemy — is likely to be himself.
Bull markets are born on pessimism, grow on skepticism, mature on optimism, and die on euphoria.
Be fearful when others are greedy and greedy when others are fearful.
Someone's sitting in the shade today because someone planted a tree a long time ago.
Only when the tide goes out do you discover who's been swimming naked.
Markets can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
Far more money has been lost by investors preparing for corrections than has been lost in corrections themselves.
The time to buy is when there's blood in the streets.
It's not whether you're right or wrong that's important, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
An economist is an expert who will know tomorrow why the things he predicted yesterday didn't happen today.
The stock market is filled with individuals who know the price of everything, but the value of nothing.
You get recessions, you have stock market declines. If you don't understand that's going to happen, then you're not ready, you won't do well in the markets.
Plan your trade and trade your plan.
Cut your losses short and let your winners run.
Bulls make money, bears make money, pigs get slaughtered.
The trend is your friend, until the end when it bends.
Nobody ever went broke taking a profit.
Sell down to the sleeping point.
Don't catch a falling knife.
Buy the rumor, sell the fact.
The market can do anything.
Amateurs want to be right. Professionals want to make money.
There is no such thing as a sure thing in the commodity markets.
Rain makes grain.
Big crops get bigger; small crops get smaller.
A short crop has a long tail.
The cure for high prices is high prices. The cure for low prices is low prices.
Sell in May and go away.
The first loss is the best loss.
Whether you think you can, or you think you can't — you're right.
Obstacles are those frightful things you see when you take your eyes off your goal.
Quality means doing it right when no one is looking.
Coming together is a beginning; keeping together is progress; working together is success.
Failure is simply the opportunity to begin again, this time more intelligently.
I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like work.
Genius is one percent inspiration and ninety-nine percent perspiration.
There is no substitute for hard work.
The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
Efficiency is doing things right; effectiveness is doing the right things.
Whenever you see a successful business, someone once made a courageous decision.
If you cannot do great things, do small things in a great way.
Don't wait. The time will never be just right.
It's fine to celebrate success but it is more important to heed the lessons of failure.
Your most unhappy customers are your greatest source of learning.
Without data, you're just another person with an opinion.
It is not necessary to change. Survival is not mandatory.
Every battle is won before it is ever fought.
He will win who knows when to fight and when not to fight.
Never let a good crisis go to waste.
Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
It ain't over till it's over.
You can observe a lot by just watching.
The future ain't what it used to be.
If you don't know where you are going, you'll end up someplace else.
I am a great believer in luck, and I find the harder I work, the more I have of it.
Chance favors the prepared mind.
Perfection is achieved, not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.
A goal without a plan is just a wish.
You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
The harder the conflict, the more glorious the triumph.
Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom must, like men, undergo the fatigue of supporting it.
Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
Character cannot be developed in ease and quiet. Only through experience of trial and suffering can the soul be strengthened.
Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement.
No one has ever drowned in sweat.
It's not the will to win that matters — everyone has that. It's the will to prepare to win that matters.
Success at anything will always come down to this: focus and effort. And we control both.
Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
It always seems impossible until it's done.
No problem can withstand the assault of sustained thinking.
Judge a man by his questions rather than his answers.
The best fertilizer is the farmer's footsteps.
You reap what you sow.
Great oaks from little acorns grow.
Look before you leap.
Strike while the iron is hot.
Many hands make light work.
Waste not, want not.
A bad penny always turns up.
Don't change horses in midstream.
You can lead a horse to water, but you can't make him drink.
Don't look a gift horse in the mouth.
While the cat's away, the mice will play.
Every cloud has a silver lining.
After the rain comes the sun.
April showers bring May flowers.
One swallow does not make a summer.
He that by the plough would thrive, himself must either hold or drive.
Sow dry and set wet.
Who has land has war.
Between saying and doing, many a pair of shoes is worn out.
Since the house is on fire let us warm ourselves.
The best word is the unspoken word.
Tomorrow is often the busiest day of the week.
More grows in the garden than the gardener sows.
A wise man changes his mind, a fool never will.
It is not enough to know how to ride; you must also know how to fall.
He who doesn't look ahead remains behind.
However long the night, the dawn will break.
Smooth seas do not make skillful sailors.
If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
Rain does not fall on one roof alone.
The best time to plant a tree is during the rainy season.
Wisdom is like a baobab tree; no one individual can embrace it.
A patient man will eat ripe fruit.
Cross the river in a crowd and the crocodile won't eat you.
When the music changes, so does the dance.
No matter how long the winter, spring is sure to follow.
What the fool does in the end, the wise man does in the beginning.
It is in the shelter of each other that the people live.
A good beginning is half the work.
There is no luck except where there is discipline.
You'll never plough a field by turning it over in your mind.
A windy day is not the day for thatching.
He who is not in the habit of riding forgets the spurs.
Willful waste makes woeful want.
What may be done at any time will be done at no time.
Trees often transplanted seldom prosper.
He who is outside the door has already a good part of his journey behind him.
Throw no stones into the well that gives you water.
He who builds according to every man's advice will have a crooked house.
Bad is never good until worse happens.
Begin to weave and God will give the thread.
The cheapest is always the most expensive.
To change and to change for the better are two different things.
God gives the nuts, but he does not crack them.
Trust, but verify.
Measure seven times, cut once.
Pray to God, but keep rowing to shore.
The slower you go, the farther you will be.
Don't sell the bear's skin before you've caught the bear.
A spring day feeds a year.
Sow a seed today; harvest a crop tomorrow.
The one who plants the tree is not the one who will enjoy its shade.
A little impatience will spoil great plans.
Dig the well before you are thirsty.
Talk doesn't cook rice.
When the winds of change blow, some people build walls and others build windmills.
A crisis is an opportunity riding the dangerous wind.
If you are patient in one moment of anger, you will escape a hundred days of sorrow.
He who asks is a fool for five minutes, but he who does not ask remains a fool forever.
Better to light a candle than to curse the darkness.
Give a man a fish and you feed him for a day; teach a man to fish and you feed him for a lifetime.
Fool me once, shame on you; fool me twice, shame on me.
Hope for the best, prepare for the worst.
Red sky at night, sailor's delight; red sky in morning, sailor's warning.
Rain before seven, fine before eleven.
When the dew is on the grass, rain will never come to pass.
Clear moon, frost soon.
Ring around the moon, rain or snow soon.
When clouds appear like rocks and towers, the earth's refreshed by frequent showers.
Mare's tails and mackerel scales make tall ships carry low sails.
When the wind is out of the east, 'tis neither good for man nor beast.
If March comes in like a lion, it will go out like a lamb.
When leaves show their undersides, be very sure that rain betides.
Knee-high by the Fourth of July.
Plant corn when oak leaves are the size of a squirrel's ear.
A dry May and a dripping June brings all things into tune.
A cold April the barn will fill.
If the rooster crows on going to bed, you may rise with a watery head.
Onion skins very thin, mild winter coming in; onion skins thick and tough, coming winter cold and rough.
A farmer's heart beats with the seasons.
A good harvest answers many questions.
The corn doesn't grow by worrying about the weather.
Never name a calf you plan to sell.
You can't plow a field with words.
The fence needs mending before the cattle get out.
Feed your land before it feeds you.
Live as if you'll die tomorrow; farm as if you'll live forever.
